title: Bare except in research_mode.py:94 swallows KeyboardInterrupt; add repo-wide bare-except lint to verify.py
current_state: core/research_mode.py line 94 has a bare 'except:' in the model retry/fallback path of the 3-agent debate. Bare except catches KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit, so Ctrl+C mid-debate is swallowed. Audit confirmed this is the only bare except in first-party code; the other ~288 swallow patterns are 'except Exception' (handled by T-254/T-255/T-264). scripts/verify.py already ast-parses every first-party .py file for its syntax gate, so the walk infrastructure exists.
target_state: research_mode.py:94 uses 'except Exception:'. verify.py fails with file:line output if any first-party file contains an ast.ExceptHandler with type=None. One bug becomes a permanent repo-wide gate; no new tooling or config.
migration_plan: 1. Edit core/research_mode.py:94: 'except:' -> 'except Exception:'. Verify the surrounding fallback behavior is unchanged.

2. In scripts/verify.py, inside the existing per-file ast.parse pass, walk the tree for ast.ExceptHandler nodes where handler.type is None; collect file:line and fail the run listing them (~10 lines). Use the exact same file inclusion/exclusion set as the existing syntax check - no new config knob.
3. Shortest test: testing/test_no_bare_except.py - feed the new lint helper a code snippet containing 'except:' and assert it is flagged; feed 'except Exception:' and assert clean (about 10 lines).
4. python scripts/verify.py must PASS - which itself proves no other bare excepts exist anywhere.
risk_notes: If the lint unexpectedly flags vendored/generated code, the fix is to keep the walk scoped to the same file list verify already syntax-checks - do not add an exclusion config. Renumbered from T-250 to T-273 on 2026-07-05: a concurrent session closed a DIFFERENT ticket under T-250 (Telegram /approve+/deny HTML parse-error fix, source: Ash live bug report) - see tickets/closed/T-250-telegram-approve-deny-html-parse-error.json. Already fully implemented and tested under the old number before the collision was discovered; content unaffected.
